meta.title: The Influence of Magnesium on Cell Division: 4. The Specificity of Magnesium

abstractHtml: SUMMARY: Magnesium exerts a specific function in (a) the formation of the Gram complex, and the activation of cell division in certain Gram-positive bacteria, and (b) activation of bacterial growth. Of a series of divalent metallic ions, only manganese is capable of partially replacing magnesium in the above processes.
